behavior_summary: Serves the 'look' (examine) event for the dagger item. When an actor examines the dagger, the script builds the literal string "dagger ", performs brief process-info/process-exclude setup (to control who receives the ensuing message), calls the item's bark routine so the dagger reports/describes itself, frees the temporary string, then suspends/returns.
No global game-state flags are read or written by this handler; its effects are local to the item/process and messaging.
Allocates a transient string, passes that string pointer to Item::bark along with the item/process pointer passed into the script, and then frees the string.
notable_control_flow: Entry: Event 0 (look) — handler executes as a process-style script acting on the item/process passed at BP+06h.
Performs a set-info / process-exclude sequence immediately before emitting the description (appears to scope or hide the bark from unintended recipients).
Calls Item::bark and consumes its return value via an implies/pop sequence (return value not used to modify global state).
Frees the temporary message string and suspends/returns after the bark call completes.
open_questions: Exact info key/id and semantics used in the set-info/process-exclude sequence: which info slot (numeric id) is being set and what precise visibility effect is intended?
Whether the process-info/process-exclude sequence modifies only temporary messaging visibility or also impacts longer-lived process state for the item (inferred as temporary here).
Whether Item::bark's return value can influence control flow in other contexts (here it is consumed but has no observed effect).

main_function.name: U8P_DAGGER::look (Func_80, Event 0)
main_function.purpose: Handle the dagger item's 'look' event by producing a short descriptive message ('dagger ') via Item::bark, while performing brief process-info/process-exclude setup and cleaning up temporary resources.
entry_conditions: Event 0: look (triggered when a player or actor examines/looks at the dagger)

function_map.name: U8P_DAGGER::look (Func_80, Event 0)
function_map.role: Process-style event handler for the dagger's 'look' event; constructs and emits the item description.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to emit/display a short text message from an item/process to a target process/player.

function_map.name: U8P_DAGGER::engine:set info / process-exclude
function_map.role: Engine operations invoked to set object/process info and exclude/include processes for messaging; used here to adjust visibility/metadata before barking.

external_calls.purpose: Emit the dagger's short description string ('dagger ') to the relevant target/process so the item 'speaks' or displays its name when examined.
Temporarily adjust process/object info and exclude/include processing so that the subsequent bark is scoped to intended recipients (visibility control).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Serves the 'look' (examine) event for the dagger item. When an actor examines the dagger, the script builds the literal string "dagger ", performs brief process-info/process-exclude setup (to control who receives the ensuing message), calls the item's bark routine so the dagger reports/describes itself, frees the temporary string, then suspends/returns.
